name: process-list
title: Process list
description: The USWDS process-list pattern: a native <ol> of long-form instructional steps, each with a numbered circular marker connected to the next by a vertical line, a heading, and body content. Distinct from StepIndicator (a compact horizontal progress tracker for a fixed wizard) — ProcessList is for reading instructions, not navigating a flow, so it adds no ARIA beyond real list/heading semantics. Each item's visible ordinal number is derived from real render order via ProcessList, not hand-typed. Optional per-item status (complete/current/upcoming) is conveyed with non-color marker cues (filled + checkmark, doubled border, lighter weight) plus a visually hidden status word. Supports substeps via a nested ProcessList (size="compact") inside an item's body.
useWhen: Long-form instructional content presented as an ordered sequence ("How to apply: 1. Gather documents … 2. Submit … 3. Await review")
Each step needs a heading plus explanatory body content, not just a short label
Optionally annotating which steps are already done, in progress, or not yet started within that reading content
avoidWhen: A compact progress tracker for a fixed multi-page wizard or checkout flow (use StepIndicator)
A plain bullet or numbered list with no per-item heading/body structure (use List)
A set of interactive choices the user selects from (use RadioGroup, ComboBox, or DropdownMenu)

keyboard: Static, non-interactive content — ProcessList and ProcessListItem never take focus and add no keyboard behavior of their own
Any interactive element placed inside an item's body (a Link, a Button) keeps its own native keyboard contract and 44px target
The current step (status="current") carries aria-current="step", matching StepIndicator's convention, so assistive technology can identify it directly
notes: Renders a real <ol>/<li> so posinset/setsize ("1 of 4", "2 of 4", …) is computed from DOM order, independent of the decorative marker or list-style CSS.

list-style: none replaces the native decimal marker with the circular marker, which silently drops list semantics from Safari/VoiceOver — role="list" is added explicitly, the same fix List's unstyled variant and IconList apply.
The circular marker is decorative (aria-hidden); its visible number is auto-derived from render order by ProcessList, not typed by hand, so reordering JSX renumbers it for free.

Status is never conveyed by color alone: complete swaps in a checkmark glyph and a filled marker, current doubles the marker's border weight, upcoming uses a lighter number weight — each also carries a visually hidden, translatable status word.
Marker and connector use flexbox's direction-aware row axis (not absolute positioning), so the whole component mirrors under dir="rtl" automatically with no rtl: overrides needed.

forced-colors:border-[CanvasText] and forced-colors:bg-[CanvasText] guarantee the marker and connector stay visible once Windows High Contrast Mode forces colors, including the filled complete state's normally-transparent border.
